checklist_items.title: Verify cryptographic authentication of Validator Agents in Mailbox
checklist_items.description: Check the Mailbox smart contract code to ensure that verification results submitted by Validator Agents are authenticated using strong cryptography (e.g., signature verification against a registered public key or identity). This prevents a malicious actor from spoofing a legitimate agent's identity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox does not sufficiently authenticate the identity of the Validator Agent submitting verification results.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong cryptographic authentication for Validator Agents interacting with the Mailbox.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, test results demonstrating signature verification.

checklist_items.title: Verify Validator Agent signs and hashes verification data
checklist_items.description: Check if the Validator Agent cryptographically hashes and signs the messages, checkpoints, and verification results it processes before sending them.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The data flow (Data Flow 20) between the Validator Agent and Mailbox is susceptible to modification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic hashing and digital signatures for all messages, checkpoints, and verification results.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of Validator Agent logic, unit tests verifying signing/hashing.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox validates cryptographic proofs
checklist_items.description: Check if the Mailbox smart contract correctly validates the cryptographic hashes and signatures of incoming messages, checkpoints, and verification results received from Validator Agents.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox does not sufficiently validate the integrity of the verification results or the underlying messages/checkpoints.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic hashing and digital signatures for all messages, checkpoints, and verification results.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review of Mailbox, unit/integration tests verifying signature/hash validation.

checklist_items.title: Verify secure channel for Validator-Mailbox communication
checklist_items.description: Check if the communication channel used by the Validator Agent to query the Mailbox (Data Flow 20) employs a secure transport protocol (e.g., HTTPS/TLS) to prevent tampering in transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The data flow (Data Flow 20) between the Validator Agent and Mailbox is susceptible to modification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure communication channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network configuration review, traffic analysis, security group rules.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox requires signed verification submissions from Validator Agents
checklist_items.description: Check if the Mailbox smart contract enforces digital signatures on all verification submissions received from Validator Agents to ensure non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox does not require cryptographically signed proofs of verification from the Validator Agent.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require digital signatures on all verification submissions by Validator Agents, ensuring non-repudiation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings confirming signature verification logic in the Mailbox contract.

checklist_items.title: Verify immutable record of Validator Agent verification submissions
checklist_items.description: Check if the system, specifically the Mailbox or associated storage, maintains an immutable record linking Validator Agents to their specific verification submissions to prevent repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There is no immutable record linking a Validator Agent to its specific verification submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require digital signatures on all verification submissions by Validator Agents, ensuring non-repudiation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings or system documentation confirming immutable storage of signed submissions linked to agents.

checklist_items.title: Verify strict access controls for Validator Agent data.
checklist_items.description: Check that only authorized processes or users can access sensitive interchain message content or verification logic processed or stored by the Validator Agent.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Access controls or logging practices within the Validator Agent are insufficient to prevent unauthorized disclosure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files, access control lists, audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify encryption of sensitive data handled by Validator Agent.
checklist_items.description: Check that sensitive interchain message content and verification-related data are encrypted both at rest (when stored) and in transit (when communicated).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent processes or stores sensitive message content or verification logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...encryption for data at rest and in transit...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files related to storage encryption, network traffic analysis results (e.g., TLS/SSL configuration).

checklist_items.title: Verify secure logging practices for Validator Agent.
checklist_items.description: Check that Validator Agent logging does not expose sensitive message content or verification details, and that log files themselves are protected with appropriate access controls.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Access controls or logging practices within the Validator Agent are insufficient to prevent unauthorized disclosure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...and secure logging practices for Validator Agents.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logging configuration files, sample log outputs, access control lists for log storage.

checklist_items.title: Verify Validator Agent redundancy and failover mechanisms.
checklist_items.description: Check if the system is designed with multiple, potentially geographically distributed, Validator Agents and if there are mechanisms (manual or automatic) to ensure message verification continues if some agents are unavailable due to DoS.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's infrastructure is vulnerable to DoS attacks (e.g., network saturation, resource exhaustion).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement redundancy, load balancing, rate limiting, and robust infrastructure for Validator Agents.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Architecture diagrams, deployment configurations, incident response playbooks for agent downtime.

checklist_items.title: Check rate limiting on Validator Agent interactions with Mailbox.
checklist_items.description: Verify if the Mailbox smart contract or an intermediary layer implements rate limiting on requests from Validator Agents to prevent resource exhaustion attacks on the Mailbox or related infrastructure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's infrastructure is vulnerable to DoS attacks (e.g., network saturation, resource exhaustion).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement... rate limiting...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review findings, API gateway configurations.
checklist_items.owner: Security Engineering Team / Smart Contract Team

checklist_items.title: Verify economic incentives for Validator Agent liveness.
checklist_items.description: Review the protocol's economic model and smart contract code (e.g., staking/slashing contracts) to confirm that Validator Agents are economically incentivized to remain online and perform their verification duties, mitigating DoS impact through financial penalties for downtime.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox relies on timely verification from Validator Agents for message finality.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Introduce economic incentives for liveness.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Protocol documentation, smart contract code review findings related to staking/slashing/rewards.
checklist_items.owner: Protocol Design Team / Smart Contract Team

checklist_items.title: Verify Validator Agent permissions adhere to least privilege
checklist_items.description: Check that the Validator Agent is granted only the minimum necessary permissions and access rights required to perform its message verification function on the Mailbox and related systems, adhering to the principle of least privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent has exploitable vulnerabilities (e.g., insecure API endpoints, misconfigured permissions).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce principle of least privilege, ... implement robust access control mechanisms for Validator Agents.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of agent roles and permissions, configuration files showing granted access rights.

checklist_items.title: Audit Validator Agent configuration and code for vulnerabilities
checklist_items.description: Conduct security audits, vulnerability scans, and configuration reviews of the Validator Agent's codebase, dependencies, and deployment environment to identify and remediate exploitable weaknesses (e.g., insecure API endpoints, credential handling).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent has exploitable vulnerabilities (e.g., insecure API endpoints, misconfigured permissions).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...conduct regular security audits, and implement robust access control mechanisms for Validator Agents.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, vulnerability scan results, configuration review findings.

checklist_items.title: Review Mailbox contract for robust verification logic against compromised agents
checklist_items.description: Analyze the Mailbox smart contract code to ensure its message and checkpoint verification logic is sufficiently robust and cannot be easily bypassed or manipulated even if a Validator Agent submitting data is compromised or acting maliciously due to elevated privileges.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox's verification logic can be bypassed or manipulated by an attacker with elevated privileges over a Validator Agent.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce principle of least privilege, conduct regular security audits, and implement robust access control mechanisms for Validator Agents.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review report highlighting relevant verification functions and their resilience against malicious input from agents.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox authenticates Relayer Agents using cryptographic signatures
checklist_items.description: Check that the Mailbox smart contract verifies cryptographic signatures on messages submitted by Relayer Agents to prevent impersonation and submission of fraudulent messages.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox does not implement strong authentication for Relayer Agents.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic signing of messages by authorized Relayer Agents and require Mailbox to verify these signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review confirming signature verification logic.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox cryptographic integrity checks for messages/proofs.
checklist_items.description: Check the Mailbox smart contract code to ensure it correctly implements and verifies cryptographic integrity mechanisms (e.g., Merkle proof verification, signature checks) for interchain messages and associated proofs received from Relayer Agents.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox's verification process does not detect all forms of message or proof tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., Merkle proofs, digital signatures) for messages and proofs, verified by the Mailbox and ISMs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report highlighting relevant verification logic and test cases.

checklist_items.title: Verify implementation of Relayer Agent economic incentives and disincentives.
checklist_items.description: Check if the protocol includes mechanisms (e.g., fees for successful relaying, slashing for malicious behavior or downtime) to incentivize Relayer Agents to operate honestly and reliably.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are insufficient incentives or penalties to ensure Relayer Agent availability and honest operation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement a decentralized network of Relayer Agents with economic incentives (e.g., fees) and disincentives (e.g., slashing) to ensure high availability and honest behavior.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation review of protocol design, smart contract code review of incentive/slashing mechanisms.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox contract implements rate-limiting or spam prevention.
checklist_items.description: Check if the Mailbox smart contract includes mechanisms (e.g., transaction limits per sender, gas limits, proof-of-work/stake requirements for message submission) to prevent a single or group of Relayers from flooding the contract with excessive or invalid transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox lacks effective rate-limiting or spam-prevention mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: The Mailbox lacks effective rate-limiting or spam-prevention mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review showing rate-limiting/spam prevention logic.

checklist_items.title: Verify strict access controls for CCIP-read Service Framework
checklist_items.description: Check that only authorized entities can access and process sensitive data within the CCIP-read Service Framework.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CCIP-read Service Framework has vulnerabilities (e.g., unauthenticated access).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls, secure logging practices, and encryption for sensitive data processed by the CCIP-read Service Framework.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration review of access control lists or authentication mechanisms.

checklist_items.title: Ensure sensitive data is not logged insecurely by CCIP-read Service Framework
checklist_items.description: Verify that the CCIP-read Service Framework's logging configuration prevents the accidental or insecure logging of sensitive information from ABI-encoded requests.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CCIP-read Service Framework has vulnerabilities (e.g., insecure logging).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls, secure logging practices, and encryption for sensitive data processed by the CCIP-read Service Framework.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Review of logging configuration and sample log outputs.

checklist_items.title: Verify encryption of sensitive data processed by CCIP-read Service Framework
checklist_items.description: Check if sensitive data within ABI-encoded requests is encrypted at rest or in transit within the CCIP-read Service Framework's processing pipeline, if applicable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The ABI-encoded request data contains sensitive information that should remain confidential.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls, secure logging practices, and encryption for sensitive data processed by the CCIP-read Service Framework.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review or configuration review demonstrating encryption implementation.

checklist_items.title: Verify multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enforced for the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.description: Check if the Deployer Account requires more than one factor for authentication (e.g., password + hardware token, biometrics) to prevent unauthorized access via compromised credentials.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system relies solely on single-factor authentication for the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong multi-factor authentication (MFA)... for the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration setting or audit log showing MFA requirement for the account.

checklist_items.title: Verify multi-party signing for critical ICA deployments.
checklist_items.description: Check if the process for deploying critical Interchain Accounts requires signatures from multiple authorized parties before execution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can intercept or modify the deployment transaction or configuration before it is submitted by the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party signing for critical deployments and cryptographic hashing of deployment configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, documentation of deployment process, configuration settings for multi-sig wallet/process.

checklist_items.title: Verify cryptographic hashing of ICA deployment configurations.
checklist_items.description: Check if deployment configurations are cryptographically hashed before being used in the deployment process to ensure their integrity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not sufficiently verify the integrity of the deployment parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party signing for critical deployments and cryptographic hashing of deployment configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings showing hashing implementation, documentation of configuration preparation process.

checklist_items.title: Verify integrity check of ICA deployment parameters using cryptographic hash.
checklist_items.description: Check if the deployment process verifies the integrity of the deployment parameters by comparing a re-calculated hash with the expected hash.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not sufficiently verify the integrity of the deployment parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party signing for critical deployments and cryptographic hashing of deployment configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings showing hash verification logic, test results demonstrating failure on hash mismatch.

checklist_items.title: Verify immutable logging of Deployer Account deployment actions
checklist_items.description: Check if all deployment actions performed by the Deployer Account are recorded in an immutable log to prevent repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not maintain immutable, verifiable records of all deployments and their initiators.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive, immutable logging and auditing for all deployment actions, including transaction hashes and timestamps.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of logging system architecture, demonstration of log immutability features.

checklist_items.title: Verify audit logs include transaction details and timestamps
checklist_items.description: Check if audit logs for Deployer Account deployment actions include necessary details like transaction hashes and timestamps to ensure actions are traceable and auditable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not maintain immutable, verifiable records of all deployments and their initiators.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive, immutable logging and auditing for all deployment actions, including transaction hashes and timestamps.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample audit logs showing transaction hashes and timestamps for deployment actions.

checklist_items.title: Ensure sensitive data related to Deployer Account is encrypted at rest.
checklist_items.description: Verify that private keys, deployment configurations, and other sensitive parameters used by or associated with the Deployer Account are stored using strong encryption.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Sensitive data (e.g., private keys, deployment secrets) related to the Deployer Account is stored or transmitted insecurely.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit, and implement strict access controls based on least privilege.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration settings of storage systems, encryption key management policies.

checklist_items.title: Ensure sensitive data related to Deployer Account is encrypted in transit.
checklist_items.description: Verify that sensitive data (private keys, configurations, parameters) transmitted to or from the environment where the Deployer Account operates is encrypted using secure protocols (e.g., TLS/SSL).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Sensitive data (e.g., private keys, deployment secrets) related to the Deployer Account is stored or transmitted insecurely.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit, and implement strict access controls based on least privilege.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network traffic analysis reports, configuration of communication channels/protocols.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access controls for Deployer Account environment.
checklist_items.description: Verify that access to systems, environments, and data stores containing sensitive information related to the Deployer Account is restricted based on the principle of least privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Access controls to the environment where the Deployer Account operates are weak.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit, and implement strict access controls based on least privilege.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control lists (ACLs), role-based access control (RBAC) configurations, access logs, security policies.

checklist_items.title: Verify RPC redundancy for ICA deployment.
checklist_items.description: Check if the Interchain Account deployment process is configured to use multiple RPC providers or includes logic for failover to alternative providers to prevent disruption from single RPC endpoint failures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The deployment process does not have built-in retry mechanisms or alternative RPC providers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust RPC redundancy
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration details, code review of deployment script, network architecture diagram.

checklist_items.title: Ensure sufficient funds/gas for Deployer Account.
checklist_items.description: Check if the Deployer Account is adequately funded and monitored to prevent deployment failures due to insufficient gas or operational funds, which could be targeted by a DoS attack.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can flood RPC endpoints or deplete the Deployer Account's operational funds.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ensure sufficient operational funds
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring setup, funding process documentation, transaction logs showing gas usage vs balance.

checklist_items.title: Monitor network health for deployment operations.
checklist_items.description: Check if monitoring systems are in place to track the health and availability of network connections and RPC endpoints used for Interchain Account deployment, enabling timely response to potential DoS conditions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can flood RPC endpoints or deplete the Deployer Account's operational funds.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: monitor network health for deployment operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboard screenshots, alert configurations, incident response procedures for network issues.

checklist_items.title: Verify Deployer Account adheres to least privilege.
checklist_items.description: Check that the Deployer Account's permissions are strictly limited to only what is necessary for deploying Interchain Accounts and cannot modify unrelated critical system parameters.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Deployer Account has permissions to modify critical system parameters unrelated to ICA deployment.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict least privilege principles for the Deployer Account...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review findings, configuration audit report.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication for Scraper Agent database connection.
checklist_items.description: Check that the Scraper Agent uses a strong authentication mechanism (e.g., mTLS, unique API key per agent instance) when connecting to the Postgres Database to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Scraper Agent's connection to the Postgres Database lacks strong mutual authentication.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication mechanisms (e.g., mTLS, API keys) for the Scraper Agent's connection to the database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files or database access control lists showing required authentication method and credentials/certificates. Audit logs demonstrating successful authenticated connections.

checklist_items.title: Verify integrity protection for data transmitted to the database.
checklist_items.description: Check if the communication channel between the Scraper Agent and the Postgres Database uses mechanisms (like TLS/SSL with integrity checks) to prevent data tampering during transmission.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The communication channel between the Scraper Agent and the database is not integrity-protected (e.g., no encryption or hashing).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement data integrity checks and encryption for data in transit and at rest.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration settings of the database connection (e.g., TLS/SSL enabled), network traffic analysis results.

checklist_items.title: Verify comprehensive and immutable logging for Scraper Agent database operations.
checklist_items.description: Check if the Postgres Database is configured to log all relevant actions performed by the Scraper Agent and if these logs are stored in a way that prevents alteration (e.g., write-once storage, separate logging service).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Database logging of Scraper Agent actions is insufficient or can be easily altered.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive, immutable logging for all database operations...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files for database logging, audit logs showing Scraper Agent actions, documentation on log storage mechanism.

checklist_items.title: Verify cryptographic signing of critical Scraper Agent database updates.
checklist_items.description: Check if the Scraper Agent cryptographically signs critical database update operations or the data being written to provide verifiable proof of origin and action.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: No cryptographic signatures or verifiable proofs are generated for database operations performed by the Scraper Agent.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...consider cryptographic signing of critical data updates.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of Scraper Agent database interaction logic, documentation on signing process, examples of signed data/operations.

checklist_items.title: Verify network firewall rules restrict access to the Postgres Database.
checklist_items.description: Check that network firewalls are configured to only allow necessary connections to the Postgres Database (Component ID 21).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database is not adequately protected by network firewalls or access controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strict network access controls...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot of firewall rules, network diagram, access control list configuration.

checklist_items.title: Verify data at rest is encrypted in the Postgres Database.
checklist_items.description: Check that data stored within the Postgres Database (Component ID 21), specifically the Indexed Blockchain Data (Asset ID 107), is encrypted at rest.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Data stored in the database is not encrypted at rest.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...encrypt data at rest...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database configuration settings, encryption key management details.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication is enforced for Postgres Database access.
checklist_items.description: Check that weak or default credentials are not used and strong authentication mechanisms are required for accessing the Postgres Database (Component ID 21).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak or default credentials are used for database access.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...and implement strong authentication for database access.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database user list, password policy configuration, authentication method configuration (e.g., requiring strong passwords, multi-factor authentication if applicable).

checklist_items.title: Implement and verify database resource limits and rate limiting.
checklist_items.description: Check that the Postgres Database is configured with appropriate resource limits (CPU, memory, connections) and rate limiting to prevent resource exhaustion during a DoS attack.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database lacks sufficient resource limits or rate limiting to withstand an attack.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database configuration screenshots, performance monitoring data under load.

checklist_items.title: Implement and verify network-level DoS protection for the database.
checklist_items.description: Check that network infrastructure (firewalls, WAF, DDoS protection services) is configured to filter malicious traffic and protect the Postgres Database from network-based DoS attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The network infrastructure protecting the database is vulnerable to DoS attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...network filtering, and ensure robust infrastructure...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network configuration diagrams, firewall rules, DDoS protection service reports.

checklist_items.title: Ensure database redundancy and failover mechanisms are in place.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Postgres Database deployment includes redundancy (e.g., replication, clustering) and failover mechanisms to maintain availability even if one instance is affected by a DoS attack.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database lacks sufficient resource limits or rate limiting to withstand an attack.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...ensure robust infrastructure with redundancy for the Postgres Database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database architecture diagrams, failover test results.

checklist_items.title: Verify Scraper Agent process privileges
checklist_items.description: Check that the operating system user or service account running the Scraper Agent process has only the minimum necessary permissions to interact with the Postgres Database and perform its indexing tasks. Excessive privileges could be exploited for privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Scraper Agent runs with more privileges than strictly necessary for its operations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply the principle of least privilege to the Scraper Agent.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files, OS user/group permissions, or process listing showing user/privileges.

checklist_items.title: Verify Postgres Database and OS patching status
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Postgres Database instance and the underlying operating system are regularly updated and patched to address known vulnerabilities, specifically those that could lead to privilege escalation. Unpatched systems are a common attack vector.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database or its underlying operating system has unpatched vulnerabilities that allow privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regularly patch and update the Postgres Database and its host system.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Patching logs, vulnerability scan reports, or system configuration details.

checklist_items.title: Verify signature verification in TokenRouter.transferRemote
checklist_items.description: Check that the TokenRouter contract correctly verifies the digital signature of the transaction initiator for `transferRemote` calls to ensure authenticity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TokenRouter does not sufficiently verify the authenticity of the transfer initiator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement ... robust signature verification for transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, test results demonstrating signature validation.

checklist_items.title: Review access control for sensitive TokenRouter functions
checklist_items.description: Examine the TokenRouter contract's access control mechanisms (e.g., onlyOwner, role checks, caller checks) to ensure only authorized entities can initiate sensitive actions like `transferRemote`.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TokenRouter does not sufficiently verify the authenticity of the transfer initiator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement ... robust signature verification for transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, access control matrix documentation.

checklist_items.title: Verify on-chain cryptographic proof verification for relayed messages.
checklist_items.description: Review the Mailbox smart contract code to ensure that all relayed messages are subject to robust cryptographic proof verification on-chain before processing, specifically checking for mechanisms that detect alterations to message content (recipient, amount, etc.).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox's proof verification mechanism is insufficient to detect message tampering by the Relayer.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust cryptographic proofs and on-chain verification mechanisms for all relayed messages.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report, unit test results demonstrating proof verification failure on tampered messages, audit findings.

checklist_items.title: Verify on-chain logging of Relayer message processing attempts.
checklist_items.description: Check if the Mailbox smart contract or associated contracts log events or state changes that record a Relayer's attempt to process a specific interchain message, including success or failure status. This log should be sufficient to prove whether a Relayer *tried* to process a message.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There is no robust on-chain logging... mechanism for Relayer actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement clear on-chain logging of Relayer actions...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, event logs from testnet/mainnet, documentation of logging mechanism.

checklist_items.title: Verify incentive/penalty mechanism for Relayer message delivery.
checklist_items.description: Check if the protocol includes a mechanism (e.g., staking, slashing, bonding) that financially incentivizes Relayers to successfully deliver messages and penalizes them for failure or non-attempt, making repudiation economically unfavorable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: ...no robust ... incentive/penalty mechanism for Relayer actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ...a robust incentive/penalty mechanism for message delivery.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, protocol documentation, economic analysis of the mechanism.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong access controls for Github Proxy and sensitive data storage.
checklist_items.description: Check that only authorized personnel/systems have access to the Github Proxy infrastructure and the storage locations of sensitive protocol configuration data and API keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github Proxy is vulnerable to attack or misconfiguration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong access controls... for the Github Proxy and sensitive data it handles.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control lists, IAM policies, audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify Post-Dispatch Hook handles insufficient payment gracefully
checklist_items.description: Check the implementation of the 'postDispatch' function in Post-Dispatch Hook smart contracts to ensure it does not revert when receiving insufficient payment. It should implement graceful handling such as logging, partial processing, or requiring exact payment upfront.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The 'postDispatch' function of a hook is configured to revert on insufficient payment.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust fee quoting mechanisms and ensure sufficient payment is enforced or handled gracefully (e.g., partial processing, refunding excess).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, test results demonstrating graceful handling of underpayment.

checklist_items.title: Verify multiple independent Relayer agents are supported and active.
checklist_items.description: Check the system architecture and operational setup to confirm that multiple independent Relayer agents can register and participate in message relaying, and that the system utilizes them effectively to avoid reliance on a single agent.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Implement multiple independent Relayer agents and a robust incentive/penalty system to ensure message delivery.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multiple independent Relayer agents and a robust incentive/penalty system to ensure message delivery.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Architectural diagrams, configuration files, operational monitoring data showing multiple active Relayers.

checklist_items.title: Audit Relayer incentive and penalty mechanisms.
checklist_items.description: Review the smart contract code and off-chain logic implementing the incentive and penalty system for Relayer agents to ensure it effectively motivates timely message delivery and deters malicious DoS behavior.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Implement multiple independent Relayer agents and a robust incentive/penalty system to ensure message delivery.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multiple independent Relayer agents and a robust incentive/penalty system to ensure message delivery.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code audit report, documentation of the incentive/penalty logic.

checklist_items.title: Verify ISM smart contract implements robust replay protection
checklist_items.description: Check the Interchain Security Module (ISM) smart contract code to ensure it uses mechanisms like nonces or unique message IDs to prevent re-execution of previously processed valid messages. Lack of such protection allows malicious actors to replay messages, potentially leading to unauthorized actions or state changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A deployed ISM does not implement sufficient replay protection.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce replay protection mechanisms (e.g., nonces, unique message IDs) within all Interchain Security Modules.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings confirming nonce/unique ID usage or test results demonstrating replay attack failure.

checklist_items.title: Verify strict access controls for deployment keys.
checklist_items.description: Check that strong access control mechanisms (e.g., role-based access, least privilege) are enforced for private keys used to sign deployment transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A developer's private key used for deployments is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls for deployment keys...
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of access control policies, audit logs of key access attempts.
